nodes.label: AI as efficiency and learning tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or frustrating tool
nodes.grounding_span: help me learn different software programs better or provide suggestions on how to implement those programs into my tasks to gain efficiency

nodes.label: AI accuracy and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: double checking the information provided by AI to confirm accuracy

nodes.label: AI memory and context awareness
nodes.grounding_span: the AI just kept providing a loop of the same instructions or suggestions, forgetting it had already provided that information

nodes.label: AI access and licensing limitations
nodes.grounding_span: the version of the AI program that my employer allows...our particular license does not allow

nodes.label: Skill maintenance vs. AI delegation
nodes.grounding_span: I choose not to atrophy skills even if I think it is likely AI may completely take them over

nodes.label: Deep institutional knowledge as AI-resistant
nodes.label_negative: Shallow or codified knowledge easily replaced by AI
nodes.grounding_span: Gaining nuanced knowledge of my business and our processes...is an example of knowledge AI is less likely to be involved in

nodes.label: Personal competence and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: I choose not to atrophy skills

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for learning and efficiency

nodes.label: Cautious trust requiring verification

nodes.label: Intentional skill maintenance despite AI capability

nodes.label: Acceptance of AI integration as inevitable
nodes.grounding_span: I imagine it will continue to become more and more engrained into my daily work

nodes.label: Step-by-step procedural learning with iterative clarification
nodes.grounding_span: I usually follow step by step so I can ask clarifying questions or alert the AI when a specific step isn't working
